logline: A museum preserves obsolete human-AI interfaces so future generations can understand the strange intimacy of early interaction paradigms.
scene: the interior of a converted textile factory repurposed as a museum, high industrial ceilings with exposed brick and iron beams, rows of vintage computing terminals and old interface devices displayed on clean white platforms, cool diffused light from skylights, a few visitors moving between exhibits, the space vast and reverent
keyProps: Converted textile factory with exposed brick and iron beams
Vintage computing terminals on clean white platforms
camera: Wide establishing shot, 35mm lens, deep depth of field, reverent institutional atmosphere
lighting: Cool diffused light from industrial skylights, clean and even with subtle warm pools around individual exhibits
productionDesign: Cool desaturated palette, 35mm film look, converted industrial space repurposed as museum, post-industrial reverence for obsolete technology, vast and contemplative
signatureTwist: a teenager sitting at a museum exhibit using a vintage 2023-era chatbot interface on an old monitor, her expression shifting from confusion to amusement, the monitor casting cool blue-white light on her face, the exhibit framed like a confession booth with curtained sides, warm overhead spot mixing with the cool screen glow
